heroHeadline: La Mayiza Consolidates ~90% of Former Chapitos Territory; Fentanyl Operations Fragmenting
heroSubtitle: 605 days of intra-cartel war following Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada's alleged kidnapping — 2,113+ homicides, 9,000+ families displaced. La Mayiza now controls approximately 90% of territory once dominated by Los Chapitos, with 'Mayito Flaco' positioned to lead a reconstituted Sinaloa Cartel. US intelligence warns of significant fragmentation of fentanyl operations as Los Chapitos — the primary producers — lose territorial control, with analysts predicting a potential shift in US drug supply toward cocaine and heroin.
footerNote: Data sourced from Noroeste, Riodoce, Proceso, InSight Crime, Reuters, AP, Animal Político, ACLED, DEA, SEDENA, SEMAR
